stage: Control Plane & Autoscaler — Design & Protocol Definition

compliance_entries.description: Every execution MUST be observable — scaling decisions are observable
compliance_entries.design_element: IAutoscaler.evaluate_load() returns ScalingSignal; IHealthSupervisor.publish_health_event() emits EventBus events

compliance_entries.description: All health events carry trace_id via EventBus payload
compliance_entries.design_element: HealthEvent dataclass includes timestamp, worker_id, event_type; EventBus topics under runtime.health.*

compliance_entries.description: All state transitions MUST be guarded — scaling state machine has guards
compliance_entries.design_element: Scaling Decision State Machine with 8 states, all transitions guarded (cooldown, hysteresis, consecutive cycles)

compliance_entries.description: Terminal states block further transitions — TERMINATE is terminal
compliance_entries.design_element: TERMINATE state is terminal; TERMINATE → COOLDOWN is the only exit; no transition from TERMINATE back to active

compliance_entries.description: Worker draining lifecycle has guarded phase transitions
compliance_entries.design_element: 5-phase draining lifecycle with guards at each phase: MARK_DRAINING → STOP_NEW_LEASES → AWAIT_COMPLETION → RELEASE_LEASES → TERMINATE

compliance_entries.description: No global state — all F2 components are per-instance
compliance_entries.design_element: IControlPlane, IAutoscaler, IHealthSupervisor, IReconciliationLoop are all instance Protocols; shared state is via EventBus only

compliance_entries.description: Scaling policy is per-instance configuration, not global
compliance_entries.design_element: ScalingPolicy dataclass is instantiated per-ControlPlane; min_workers, max_workers, cooldown_sec are instance fields

compliance_entries.description: Determinism — same load + same policy → same scaling signal
compliance_entries.design_element: IAutoscaler.evaluate_load() is pure: no random, no side effects; IReconciliationLoop.compare_desired() computes deterministic delta

compliance_entries.description: Reversibility — scaling actions are reversible via scale_down/drain
compliance_entries.design_element: SCALE_UP reversed by SCALE_DOWN; worker provisioning reversed by drain+terminate; RULE 2 exception: individual drain is irreversible but system-level reversibility via new worker provisioning
IHealthSupervisor.assess_degradation() allows transition from MINOR/MAJOR back to NONE; recovery events published as worker.health.recovered

compliance_entries.description: Idempotency — all F2 operations are idempotent
compliance_entries.design_element: drain_worker: already DRAINING → return existing receipt; trigger_eviction: already TERMINATED → return receipt; apply_scaling: same target+current → no-op; register_worker: duplicate manifest → return existing

compliance_entries.description: Lease integrity — terminate requires RELEASE_LEASES first
compliance_entries.design_element: Draining lifecycle section: RELEASE_LEASES phase before TERMINATE; timeout safeguard force-releases leases if AWAIT_COMPLETION exceeds max_drain_wait_sec

compliance_entries.description: Reconciliation interval strategy — observe 5s, evaluate 15s, act 30s
compliance_entries.design_element: Tiered timer approach with OBSERVE/EVALUATE/ACT phases; timer diagram in integration blueprint
MARK_DRAINING → STOP_NEW_LEASES → AWAIT_COMPLETION → RELEASE_LEASES → TERMINATE; each phase has guard and expected duration

compliance_entries.description: Oscillation prevention — cooldown and hysteresis mandatory
compliance_entries.design_element: 4 hysteresis guards: consecutive cycle requirement (2x UP/DOWN), hysteresis dead-band, cooldown window, scale step limiting

compliance_entries.description: Control Plane protocol — reconcile, enforce_policy, publish_state, drain_worker
compliance_entries.design_element: IControlPlane with 4 methods matching §15.9.1: reconcile(), enforce_policy(), publish_state(), drain_worker()

compliance_entries.description: Integration with F1 UnifiedRuntime — register_worker, scale, observe
compliance_entries.design_element: Integration blueprint maps F1 API methods to F2 consumers with 3 connection points; EventBus topics under runtime.scaling.* and runtime.worker.*

compliance_entries.description: Integration with D8 Service Mesh — EventBus, FailureMatrix, Scheduler, LeaseManager
compliance_entries.design_element: 5 D8 connection points: EventBus for all F2 communication, FailureMatrix for failure classification, ExecutionScheduler for drain, ExecutionLeaseManager for lease release, ExecutionStateStore for state queries
